claim_text: Claims footballer from Denmark Christian Eriksen's collapse at the Euros was linked to Covid-19.
title: Claims Christian Eriksen's collapse was linked to Covid-19 Vaccine are FFS
verdict_text: It is incorrect to claim that Eriksen had received any Covid 19 vaccine prior to his collapse during Denmark’s match against Finland. Inter Milan officials have confirmed he had not been vaccinated and Denmark’s team doctor confirmed that the footballer suffered a cardiac arrest. Due to his age and health profile Eriksen would not have been eligible for a vaccine in either Italy or Denmark prior to the game. The original source of the claim has since confirmed he does not have evidence to back it up.

claim_text: 95% of new workers are foreigners.
title: Foreigners working in the UK - Full Fact
verdict_text: Incorrect. These employment figures don’t tell us the number of new workers, only the overall change in employment. The claim also defines “foreigners” as all people born outside the UK, including those who are UK citizens. About 450,000 more people in the UK were employed in July-September 2016 than had been the year before, and roughly half of the increase was due to an increase in the number of non-British citizens.

claim_text: The Delta variant is more harmless than earlier variants, but double-vaccinated people are more likely to die from the Delta variant than unvaccinated people.
title: Delta variant not more dangerous for vaccinated people
verdict_text: Taken out of context. The data cited are correct, but the claim that the Delta variant is less dangerous but more deadly for vaccinated people cannot be concluded from them. Firstly, figures from different phases of the pandemic cannot be compared with each other, and secondly, the proportion of unvaccinated people among corona deaths only appears to be falling.

claim_text: The deficit has come down under the Conservatives.

verdict_text: This is correct, the deficit has fallen from 10% of GDP to 2.3% between 2010 and 2017, but the government is far off its original plans for a reaching a surplus.

claim_text: There are more hospital beds.
title: NHS: More beds, more doctors, and more flu vaccines? - Full Fact
verdict_text: Incorrect. The number of beds available in NHS hospitals in England has been falling for the last thirty years, something experts put down to medical innovations. 1,300 fewer beds were used by patients fit to be discharged in November 2017 compared to the year before.

claim_text: Amid the Coronavirus outbreak, a claim has surfaced on the internet according to which as many as 150 health workers have been quarantined at Delhi's premier Max Hospital in Saket.
title: Did COVID-19 spread at a top Delhi private hospital, here is the fact check | Newsmobile
verdict_text: NewsMobile spoke to senior doctors from the hospital who denied the claim and called it FAKE. The hospital in a statement clarified that 39 of its staff members, including doctors and nurses have been in quarantine within a separate and isolated wing in the hospital after two patients tested positive for coronavirus.

claim_text: The asylum initial decision backlog is down by 17,000.
title: The total asylum initial decision backlog has increased since December - Full Fact
verdict_text: This is not correct in terms of the total number of asylum cases awaiting an initial decision, which has increased since December 2022. However the “legacy backlog”, which the government defines as asylum applications made before 28 June 2022 still awaiting an initial decision, has fallen by almost 18,000 since December.

claim_text: Two thirds of asylum-seekers who arrived here since 2018 claiming to be unaccompanied children were found to be adults.
title: Claim two-thirds of child asylum seekers are actually adults is false - Full Fact
verdict_text: This is not true, and is based on a misinterpretation of Home Office statistics. According to official figures, around 20% of people who said they were unaccompanied children while claiming asylum in the UK were later found to be adults between January 2018 and March 2022.
